title: ST_558_HW_3
text:
Question 3
#1. Read in the White Wine Excel file

library(readxl)
white_wine <- read_excel("~/Desktop/ST 558/ST 558 HW3/white-wine.xlsx", sheet = 1)
white_wine 

# A tibble: 4,898 × 12
   `fixed acidity` `volatile acidity` `citric acid` `residual sugar` chlorides
             <dbl>              <dbl>         <dbl>            <dbl>     <dbl>
 1               7               0.27          0.36             20.7     0.045
 2              63               0.3           0.34              1.6     0.049
 3              81               0.28          0.4               6.9     0.05 
 4              72               0.23          0.32              8.5     0.058
 5              72               0.23          0.32              8.5     0.058
 6              81               0.28          0.4               6.9     0.05 
 7              62               0.32          0.16              7       0.045
 8               7               0.27          0.36             20.7     0.045
 9              63               0.3           0.34              1.6     0.049
10              81               0.22          0.43              1.5     0.044
# ℹ 4,888 more rows
# ℹ 7 more variables: `free sulfur dioxide` <dbl>,
#   `total sulfur dioxide` <dbl>, density <dbl>, pH <dbl>, sulphates <dbl>,
#   alcohol <dbl>, quality <dbl>


#2. Rename columns using Sheet 2

new_names <- read_excel("~/Desktop/ST 558/ST 558 HW3/white-wine.xlsx", sheet = 2)

colnames(white_wine) <- new_names[[1]]

#3. Add type column for white wine

library(dplyr)

white_wine <- white_wine |> 
  mutate(type = "white")

#4. Read in the Red Wine CSV

library(readr)
red_wine <- read_delim("~/Desktop/ST 558/ST 558 HW3/red-wine.csv", 
                       delim = ";",
                       show_col_types = FALSE)
colnames(red_wine) <- new_names[[1]]

red_wine <- red_wine |> 
              mutate(type = "red")

#5. Combine both datasets

wine <- dplyr::bind_rows(white_wine, red_wine)

#6-9. Combined data object to filter the data

wine |> 
  # Filter high quality and reasonable alcohol 
  filter(quality > 6.5 & alcohol < 132) |>
  # Sort highest quality to lowest
  arrange(desc(quality)) |>
  # Select acid columns, alcohol, type, quality
  dplyr::select(contains("acid"), alcohol, type, quality) |>
  # Add mean and sd of alcohol for each quality level
  group_by(quality) |>
  mutate(
    mean_alcohol = mean(alcohol),
    sd_alcohol = sd(alcohol)
  )

# A tibble: 1,206 × 8
# Groups:   quality [3]
   fixed_acidity volatile_acidity citric_acid alcohol type  quality mean_alcohol
           <dbl>            <dbl>       <dbl>   <dbl> <chr>   <dbl>        <dbl>

 2            66             0.36        0.29     124 white       9        122. 
 3            74             0.24        0.36     125 white       9        122. 
 4            69             0.36        0.34     127 white       9        122. 
 5            71             0.26        0.49     129 white       9        122. 
 6            62             0.66        0.48     128 white       8         94.1
 7            62             0.66        0.48     128 white       8         94.1
 8            68             0.26        0.42     105 white       8         94.1

10            67             0.23        0.31     107 white       8         94.1

title: ST_558_HW_3
text:
Question 4
#1. Connect to the database and list all tables

library(DBI)

con <- dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), "~/Desktop/ST 558/ST 558 HW3/lahman.db")

dbListTables(con)

 [1] "AllstarFull"         "Appearances"         "AwardsManagers"     
 [4] "AwardsPlayers"       "AwardsShareManagers" "AwardsSharePlayers" 
 [7] "Batting"             "BattingPost"         "CollegePlaying"     
[10] "Fielding"            "FieldingOF"          "FieldingOFsplit"    
[13] "FieldingPost"        "HallOfFame"          "HomeGames"          
[16] "LahmanData"          "Managers"            "ManagersHalf"       
[19] "Parks"               "People"              "Pitching"           
[22] "PitchingPost"        "Salaries"            "Schools"            
[25] "SeriesPost"          "Teams"               "TeamsFranchises"    
[28] "TeamsHalf"           "battingLabels"       "fieldingLabels"     
[31] "pitchingLabels"     


#2. Using the tbl() function and dplyr to return all of the data

library(dbplyr)


Attaching package: 'dbplyr'


The following objects are masked from 'package:dplyr':

    ident, sql

tbl(con, "Teams") |>
  filter(yearID == 2015 ) |>
  collect()

#3. Using the sql() function

library(dbplyr)

tbl(con, "Teams") |>
  filter(yearID == 2015 ) |>
  collect()

# A tibble: 30 × 48
   yearID lgID  teamID franchID divID  Rank     G Ghome     W     L DivWin WCWin
    <int> <chr> <chr>  <chr>    <chr> <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <chr>  <chr>

 2   2015 NL    ATL    ATL      E         4   162    81    67    95 N      N

10   2015 AL    DET    DET      C         5   161    81    74    87 N      N

#   X2B <int>, X3B <int>, HR <int>, BB <int>, SO <int>, SB <int>, CS <int>,
#   HBP <int>, SF <int>, RA <int>, ER <int>, ERA <dbl>, CG <int>, SHO <int>,
#   SV <int>, IPouts <int>, HA <int>, HRA <int>, BBA <int>, SOA <int>, E <int>,
#   DP <int>, FP <dbl>, name <chr>, park <chr>, attendance <int>, BPF <int>,
#   PPF <int>, teamIDBR <chr>, teamIDlahman45 <chr>, teamIDretro <chr>


#4. Inducted players only

hof <- tbl(con, sql("
          SELECT playerID, yearID, category
          From HallOfFame 
          WHERE inducted = 'Y'
          ")) |>
        collect()
hof

# A tibble: 340 × 3
   playerID  yearID category         
   <chr>      <int> <chr>            
 1 cobbty01    1936 Player           
 2 ruthba01    1936 Player           
 3 wagneho01   1936 Player           
 4 mathech01   1936 Player           
 5 johnswa01   1936 Player           
 6 lajoina01   1937 Player           
 7 speaktr01   1937 Player           
 8 youngcy01   1937 Player           
 9 bulkemo99   1937 Pioneer/Executive
10 johnsba99   1937 Pioneer/Executive
# ℹ 330 more rows


#5. Add nameFirst and nameLast from People

hof_names <- tbl(con, sql("
              SELECT h.playerID, h.yearID, h.category, p.nameFirst, p.nameLast
              From HallOfFame h
              JOIN People p ON h.playerID = p.playerID
              WHERE h.inducted = 'Y'
              ")) |>
            collect()
hof_names

# A tibble: 338 × 5
   playerID  yearID category          nameFirst nameLast 
   <chr>      <int> <chr>             <chr>     <chr>    
 1 cobbty01    1936 Player            Ty        Cobb     
 2 ruthba01    1936 Player            Babe      Ruth     
 3 wagneho01   1936 Player            Honus     Wagner   
 4 mathech01   1936 Player            Christy   Mathewson
 5 johnswa01   1936 Player            Walter    Johnson  
 6 lajoina01   1937 Player            Nap       Lajoie   
 7 speaktr01   1937 Player            Tris      Speaker  
 8 youngcy01   1937 Player            Cy        Young    
 9 bulkemo99   1937 Pioneer/Executive Morgan    Bulkeley 
10 johnsba99   1937 Pioneer/Executive Ban       Johnson  
# ℹ 328 more rows


#6. Managers win/loss records and win percentage

managers <- tbl(con, "Managers") |>
                select(playerID, G, W, L) |>
                group_by(playerID) |>
                summarize(G_managed = sum(G, na.rm = TRUE),
                  Total_W = sum(W, na.rm = TRUE),
                  Total_L = sum(L, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
                collect() |>
                mutate(win_pct = Total_W/G_managed) |>
                arrange(desc(win_pct))
managers

# A tibble: 749 × 5

10 tamarjo01         1       1       0       1

#7. Join two previous table

hof_names |>
  inner_join(managers, by = "playerID")
